question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a flood of requests with crafted URLs that cause `res.locals[requestPathLabel]` to be set to an extremely long string on every request, exhausting heap memory across all three routers (AccountRoutes, BlockRoutes, ContractRoutes) and crashing the Node.js process, causing total API unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a ReDoS condition by crafting a URL path with thousands of trailing slashes that causes the `.replace(/\\/+$/g, '')` regex in `recordRequestPath` to consume unbounded CPU time, blocking the event loop and preventing all subsequent requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests where `req.route?.path` is undefined (unmatched route) and `res.locals[requestPathLabel]` is never set, causing downstream middleware that depends on `requestPathLabel` to throw unhandled exceptions that crash the server?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `req.baseUrl` value of extreme length combined with a long `req.route.path` such that the string concatenation `${req.baseUrl}${req.route.path}` in `recordRequestPath` allocates a massive string object per request, causing a memory exhaustion DoS when sent at high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/index.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: recordRequestPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where `req.route` is a non-null object but `req.route.path` is a non-string type (e.g., an object or array injected via prototype pollution), causing the `path.startsWith(apiPrefix)` call to throw a TypeError that bypasses error handling and crashes the process?
